reason: 4 P1 defects found: illusory coverage for binary relations as Set(α×α), weak Denumerable closure, W17 L06 introduces ≃ before it is taught, missing non-reflexive counterexample in W15. All must be resolved before plan passes.
p1Defects: Binary relation as Set(α×α) has illusory coverage — mentioned conceptually but never practiced. Coverage map requires connecting both representations.
Denumerable has weak closure despite core status — introduced in W25 but never retrieved, integrated in a boss, or transferred.
W17 L06 introduces ≃ notation (via Setoid.quotientKerEquivRange) before Equiv is taught in W22. Dependency ordering violation.
Missing non-reflexive counterexample in W15 — learner sees non-symmetric (L04) and non-transitive (L05) but never non-reflexive. Three-property framework incomplete.
p2Defects: W04 has diffuse center of gravity — bundles indexed ops with Set.prod, Set.Nonempty, Set.powerset.
Set.Nonempty is core but has no boss integration (G='—' in closure table).
Encodable is core but has no transfer stage (T='—' in closure table).
rintro/rcases are core LEAN-axis items but never explicitly taught — left as hidden discovery items.
recommendations: Add 1-2 levels to W15 for binary relations as Set(α×α) and Set.diagonal, with translation between α→α→Prop and Set(α×α) representations.
Resolve W17 L06 / Equiv dependency: drop L06 from W17 (move to W28) or make it a preview level not requiring Equiv proficiency.
Add a non-reflexive counterexample level to W15 (e.g., ¬ Reflexive (· ≠ ·)).
Strengthen Denumerable closure: add to W25 boss or a W27 level, or demote to supporting in coverage map.
Split W04 to separate indexed operations from Set.prod/Nonempty/powerset, or document why mixed center is acceptable.
Add Encodable transfer to W27 with a fresh type.
Teach rintro/rcases explicitly at W07 rather than leaving as hidden discovery items.
notes: Plan architecture is sound — proof-move map is explicit and complete, transfer plan is substantive, inventory release is thorough. All P1 fixes are localized (~4-5 added levels across existing worlds). No restructuring needed.
